titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Pirelli Strike Fliers and Newspapers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 10, 2025 at 23:16

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains fliers, handbills, newspapers, and periodicals by or relating to workers on strike at the Pirelli tire plant in Bicocca and other factories in Milan, Italy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Librisenzadata on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Fliers, 1956–1976. II. Newspapers, 1962–1974.

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: In 1968, workers at the Pirelli tire factory in the Bicocca district of Milan, Italy formed the Comitato unitario di base (CUB), an autonomous, unitary rank-and-file committee independent from the established Italian trade unions. The Pirelli CUB organized a series of wildcat strikes, slowdowns, and stoppages that resulted in a protracted period of labor disputes from September 1968 through December 1969. Other Milanese factories and industries mobilized in the fall of 1969 during a strike wave known as the "Hot Autumn." The major trade unions CGIL, CISL, and UIL became involved with the striking workers, as did the Italian Communist Party (PCI) and other left-wing organizations. Pirelli factory workers continued to organize through the 1970s. Pirelli later closed its Bicocca plant in 1985.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pirelli Strike Fliers and Newspapers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains fliers, handbills, newspapers, and periodicals by or relating to workers on strike at the Pirelli tire plant in Bicocca and other factories in Milan, Italy. Materials document rank-and-file committees, trade unions, and left-wing political organizations involved in the Milanese labor movement from 1968 until the mid-1970s. Included are materials by Confederazione generale italiana del lavoro (CGIL); Confederazione italiana sindacati lavoratori (CISL); Unione italiana del lavoro (UIL); Federazione unitaria CGIL-CISL-UIL; Lotta continua; Partito comunista italiano (PCI); and Partito socialista italiano di unità proletaria (PSIUP).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Pirelli Strike Fliers and Newspapers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Partito socialista italiano di unità proletaria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains fliers and handbills created by the Pirelli Comitato unitario di base (CUB) and other trade unions and organizations active in Milan, such as CGIL, CISL, UIL, Lotta continua, PCI, and PSIUP. Other groups represented include unaffiliated groups of workers, works councils, trade union representatives, and workers involved in a trial against Pirelli.

did.unittitle: Assemblea operai uniti Pirelli e Assemblea operaia unitaria
Contratto collettivo nazionale del lavoro, Pirelli
Direzione studi economici e programmazione, Industrie Pirelli
Federazione italiana lavoratori chimici e affini (FILCEA-CGIL), SILG [Sindacato italiano lavoratori della gomma]
Federazione italiana lavoratori chimici e petrolieri (FILCEP-CGIL), SILG [Sindacato italiano lavoratori della gomma]
Gruppo di lavoratori (richiesta di essere dispensati dagli scioperi)
Gruppo di studio operai impiegati, SIT-Siemens
Partito socialista italiano di unità proletaria (PSIUP)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains newspapers produced for workers in Milan by trade unions and organizations such as CISL, PCI, and PSIUP.

did.unittitle: Il battistrada . Supplemento a Milano sindacale a cura della SAS CISL
La Bicocca . Periodico sindacale dei lavoratori della gomma
La fabbrica . Periodico di attualità a cura della sezione del PCI Libero Temolo (Pirelli)
Il lavoratore metallurgico . Periodico dei metallurgici milanesi
Lotta unita . Periodico unitario lavoratori chimici ed affini di Milano
Mondo nuovo . Settimanale del Partito socialista italiano di unità proletaria
Noi giovani . Foglio curato e realizzato dal gruppo giovani della Federchimici-CISL-Pirelli Bicocca
